summary: Excellent audience recording captured on Sony ECM220T mics, featuring a tight 1982 performance highlighted by a funky Touch of Grey
review: Fans rave about the crystal‑clear audience capture made from high‑quality Sony ECM220T microphones, noting the mix is generally superb though vocals sit forward and Phil’s bass sounds a bit muffled. Musically the show is praised as one of the band’s tightest of ’82 with Jerry delivering a funky vocal on Touch of Grey, despite a few comments about a rushed ending.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews are 5‑star, highlighting outstanding performance and superb tape quality; minor mix complaints lower the recording rating slightly but overall the show/recording combo is judged excellent.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Recorded from audience cassettes using Sony ECM220T mics – crisp and clear overall. Some listeners note vocals are prominent and Phil’s bass sounds muffled/under‑driven in the mix.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Tight, inspiring playing throughout with an amazing set list; a few reviewers felt the show ended a bit rushed.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for delivering a funky vocal on Touch of Grey and overall strong guitar work.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass described as mushy, flat and buried in the mix due to instrument change.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent's Yamaha CP‑70 piano sounds weak and flat compared with his earlier Rhodes tone.
